stories.title: Love Island SPOILER: 'Gameplay' accusations rock the villa as Maya Jama puts Islanders in the hot seat
stories.summary: By on 23rd July 2026 in By on 23rd July 2026 in The events of The Grafties have boiled over into another episode of Love Island , as accusations of 'gameplay' have unsettled the Islanders. In a first look at Thursday's episode, Priya questions Julia over her thoughts on her connection with Aidan. She asks: 'Do you think I’m genuinely with Aidan as a gameplay, to get to the final? ' Julia admits: 'I do think you’ve developed a connection. He would pick you over any new bombshell as that would guarantee the final. ' Priya and Yasmin, Love Island. Pic: ITV The conversation shifts to the wider dynamics in the Villa.

stories.title: [TUY8S2.2] Dust Storm Event, Creator Collaboration & Updates
stories.summary: CONTENTS • Dust Storm Event • Classified Assignment • Realism Mode • Creator Collaboration • Paradise Lost Update • Stats And Resources Multiplier Events • Changes and Fixes DUST STORM EVENT Summer is in full swing, and it’s time to experience New York as you have never seen it before. From July 21 to August 11 , defeat powerful enemies, collect exclusive loot and navigate hazardous weather conditions in the Dust Storm Event . All players who log in to the game during the event period will be granted the Parched Mask as a free reward. As part of the celebrations, access to the Warlords of New York expansion will be free for all players for the duration of the event.

stories.title: Italy confess Carlo Ancelotti was their dream appointment before Pep Guardiola in manager hunt
stories.summary: Italy have confirmed that Carlo Ancelotti was their first choice to become the country's next national team manager before holding talks with Pep Guardiola, as the Italian Football Federation (FIGC) continues its search for a new head coach following another disappointing World Cup campaign. The Azzurri have been without a permanent manager since Gennaro Gattuso resigned in April after Italy failed to qualify for a third consecutive FIFA World Cup, extending one of the most difficult periods in the nation's modern football history.

stories.title: How to see the Perseids meteor shower this summer
stories.summary: Up to 100 meteors per hour can typically be seen during the Perseids shower. July 21, 2026, 4:43 PM It's the time of the summer when the Perseids meteor shower lights up the night sky. The Perseids occur every year from about July 17 to Aug. 23 and typically peak around mid-August, according to the American Meteor Society . This year, the Perseids are expected to peak between Aug. 12 and 13, according to AMS. The Perseids meteor shower is known for its frequency of meteors – capable of producing between 50 and 100 visible meteors per hour in a clear dark sky, according to NASA .
sources: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

stories.title: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over past jibe
stories.summary: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over 'awful' comment in the past Updated / Wednesday, 22 Jul 2026 18:15 Getty (L-R) Miquita Oliver and Fearne Cotton - Oliver was let go from her radio job in 2005 following an interview with NME where she was asked how she would improve Top of the Pops and replied that the programme should get rid of Cotton Presenter Miquita Oliver has apologised to Fearne Cotton after Oliver was sacked from BBC Radio 1 in 2005 for making an "awful" comment about her fellow broadcaster.

stories.title: Live updates: Oil tops $100 a barrel as Houthi attack on Saudi vessels in Red Sea marks new escalation
stories.summary: Oil prices surged past $100 a barrel for the first time since May after Houthi rebels attacked Saudi vessels in the Red Sea. The U.S. military launched its 13th straight night of airstrikes on Iran following President Trump’s refusal to negotiate a ceasefire. The House voted to block further military action without congressional approval, though the Senate rejected the measure.

stories.title: Fortnite Cube Sprites Release Date Explained
stories.summary: What are the Fortnite Cube Sprites? This is a new feature that we’re expecting to arrive in the next Fortnite update. It’ll be a brand-new variant for each of the sprites. joining Holofoil, Gold, and Galaxy as a new type of collectible. But how exactly will they work? Chapter 7 Season 3 has gone all in on Sprites. A little collectible you can track down in each game. Giving you unique Sprite powers. But also, something to extract and collect, so you can use them in the cosmetics in the Battle Plus. Plus, the bragging rights of just having a really rare sprite. So how do the Fortnite Cube Sprites work and when is their release date going to be? What are Fortnite Cube Sprites?

stories.title: World Instant Jasmine Tea Powder - Market Analysis, Forecast, Size, Trends and Insights
stories.summary: Abstract According to the latest IndexBox report on the global Instant Jasmine Tea Powder market , the market enters 2026 with broader demand fundamentals, more disciplined procurement behavior, and a more regionally diversified supply architecture. The world market for Instant Jasmine Tea Powder is entering a structurally supported growth phase, with demand projected to expand at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 5. 7% from 2026 to 2035, lifting the market index to 170 (2025=100). This trajectory reflects a convergence of consumer behavior shifts, foodservice channel maturation, and ingredient innovation.

stories.title: TV tonight: documentary reveals the shocking truth behind model family blogger Ruby Franke
stories.summary: A new BBC Two documentary tonight reveals how model family blogger Ruby Franke’s YouTube channel, once earning £100,000 a month, collapsed after her 2023 arrest for child abuse. Footage shows her emaciated son escaping to call for help, exposing the fraud behind the wholesome facade watched by nearly three million subscribers. The one-hour film airs at 9pm, detailing how life coaching and staged perfection hid a harrowing reality.

stories.title: Derry's European fate in the balance after narrow defeat in Croatia
stories.summary: Derry City face a 1-0 deficit ahead of their Europa Conference League second leg against HNK Rijeka next Thursday. Croatian international Toni Fruk scored the only goal in a rain-soaked first leg in Croatia. Derry will need to overturn the deficit at the Ryan McBride Brandywell Stadium to progress.

stories.title: Tyla Turns Up the Heat With ‘Is It Love’ VEVO Performance
stories.summary: Tyla is keeping the momentum sky-high ahead of the arrival of her eagerly awaited sophomore album, ‘ A*POP . ’ The Grammy winner has unveiled an electrifying VEVO Official Live Performance of her latest single, ‘ Is It Love . ’ Full story below… The sleek visual puts her captivating vocals and effortless stage presence front and center, offering fans a fresh take on one of the project’s standout cuts. The performance marks the first in a series of four VEVO live sessions tied to the album rollout. Just hours before the LP lands, the release serves as the perfect appetizer before ‘A*POP’ arrives July 24 .

stories.title: Maya Jama Teases "Shakeup" To 'Love Island UK' Season 13 Finale: "There's Going To Be A Different Format For The Final"
stories.summary: Maya Jama is the host of Love Island UK, which is wrapping up its summer season in the Mallorca villa. The UK’s ITV2 dating series streams on Hulu in the United States, and Jama’s profile continues to grow among American audiences. The Disney-owned streamer has plastered billboards of Jama all over major cities promoting the show, with the presenter telling Deadline it’s “wild. ” “It’s been amazing. I get sent photos of those billboards, which is wild to be honest,” Jama told us in a recent interview. “I’ve got some friends that live [in America], and they’re like, ‘Look! There’s your big head next to Kim Kardashian. ’ So that’s crazy.

stories.title: Trump scoffs at ‘affordability’ as he touts economic policies at Georgia rally
stories.summary: Donald Trump scoffed at the word “affordability” as a political message, describing it at a campaign rally on Wednesday as a term concocted by Democratic political consultants to criticize his presidency. Yet even as the president dismissed the notion of affordability, he devoted much of his speech at Wheeler high school to touting the bread-and-butter benefits of his economic policies. “So I just want you to remember that if you’re paying a little bit more – a lot of it’s come down now, we got it coming down very rapidly actually,” Trump said in Marietta, an Atlanta suburb, casting higher prices today as a lingering effect of the Biden administration.

stories.title: Tommy White — Cobb County
stories.summary: Follow Tommy White Get notified by email when this case is updated. I agree to receive email updates for this case. Send me other news stories that may be of interest.

stories.title: Ajax Turner Senior Center board splits, takes conflicting votes, starts over
stories.summary: After a set of meetings in which two versions of the Senior Citizens Center Board took conflicting actions, the City of Clarksville provided an update on its plans. CLARKSVILLE, TN ( CLARKSVILLE NOW ) – After a set of meetings in which two versions of the Senior Citizens Center Board took conflicting actions, with resignations and restarts, the City of Clarksville provided an update on its plans, stating it will move forward with taking over the Ajax Turner Senior Center operations. But several questions are still unanswered. The City of Clarksville said Monday the Senior Citizens Center at 953 Clark St.

stories.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
stories.summary: The US is imposing new tariffs of 10% to 12.5% on imports from around 60 trading partners, including the UK, China, the EU, Canada, Japan and India, starting Friday. The move targets countries accused of failing to stop forced labour, escalating trade tensions after a Supreme Court ruling blocked earlier tariffs. The White House says the duties aim to address human rights abuses and unfair trade practices.

stories.title: Men Flock to Microdramas, Romance Stories Rule and Sunday Nights are Prime Time for Viewing
stories.summary: Microdrama viewership has surged 28-fold in two years, with romance stories dominating and Sunday nights between 8-10 p.m. drawing the biggest crowds. Over two-thirds of users, now including more men, watch these short-form series, and heavy viewers show surprising openness to AI-generated content. The trend reflects a younger, more engaged audience reshaping digital entertainment.

stories.title: Javier Bardem Reveals the Perks of Working With Wife Penelope Cruz: ‘There’s a Level of Trust’
stories.summary: Javier Bardem revealed the advantages of collaborating with his wife, Penélope Cruz, during a screening for Cape Fear in New York on July 21. Their upcoming film Bunker, set for U.S. release in early 2027, follows an architect whose marriage is strained after he designs a billionaire’s bunker. Bardem praised Cruz’s trust and understanding, saying it streamlined their work.

stories.title: Heatwave anxiety can make you feel 'incredibly helpless' - here's how to manage it
stories.summary: Extreme heat can take a toll on mental health - and young people and women are among the groups most at risk, according to one study. At the peak of the June heatwave, the warmest June on record in England, all Tavia Watts could think about was how hot it was in her two- and five-year-old's bedroom - above 30C in some cases. "During the heatwave, I've got a high level of anxiety all the time and I'm pretty much useless," she told the BBC. "I've had to leave bedtime when my husband reads [my kids] stories to have a little cry and to calm down, because it's just too hot in their room and I'm afraid for them.

stories.title: UK complacent about war threat, warns defence boss
stories.summary: The head of Europe’s largest defence firm, BAE Systems, warns the UK is dangerously complacent about rising foreign attack risks. Dr Charles Woodburn says threats are at their highest in decades and calls for faster spending to meet the 3.5% of GDP defence target by 2035. He also cautions that autonomous weapons could soon be deployed by states ignoring human control rules.

stories.title: ‘Marvelous Mrs. Maisel’ Set Decorator Mary Kate Golding Fatally Stabbed in N.Y.
stories.summary: A set decorator for The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel and other shows was fatally stabbed in her Queens, New York, apartment on July 21, 2026. Mary Kate Golding, 34, was pronounced dead at the scene, and her husband, Joseph Azzaretto, was later found dead by hanging in Astoria Park. Police suspect a murder-suicide after responding to two separate 911 calls that morning.

stories.title: How Arantxa Sanchez Vicario’s $41 million tennis fortune ‘vanished’ as her life fell apart
stories.summary: Arantxa Sanchez Vicario, former World No.1 tennis star, claims her $41 million fortune was drained by her ex-husband, leaving her penniless. A Spanish court found both guilty of fraud in January 2024 for hiding assets to avoid debts to Banque de Luxembourg. Sanchez Vicario avoids jail by paying half her earnings to the bank.

stories.title: ‘Ebenezer: A Christmas Carol’ Trailer: Johnny Depp Transforms Into Grouchy, Snowball-Throwing Scrooge
stories.summary: Johnny Depp stars as Ebenezer Scrooge in the dark holiday reimagining Ebenezer: A Christmas Carol, his first major Hollywood role since his 2022 defamation trial. Directed by Ti West, the trailer shows Scrooge haunted by spirits and hurling snowballs in a bleak, snow-covered London. The film arrives as Depp returns to the screen following his high-profile legal battle with Amber Heard.

stories.title: Scientists find building blocks of birdsong's 'spectacular diversity'
stories.summary: Scientists in France have identified eight fundamental sound motifs—three trills, three whistles, chaotic notes and harmonies—that form the basis of all birdsong worldwide. Analysing 116,000 songs from over 3,000 species, researchers say these motifs combine to create the vast diversity of birdsong. The team now plans to use AI to explore how these patterns evolve across different regions.

stories.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
stories.summary: A top US science adviser accused China’s Moonshot AI of large-scale theft, saying it used weaker models to extract capabilities from Anthropic’s Fable AI for its K3 system. The adviser also claimed Moonshot accessed restricted Nvidia servers to train its models, a charge the company has not commented on. The K3 model was unveiled last week, drawing global attention for closing the gap with Western AI systems.

stories.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
stories.summary: An Indonesian court has convicted 19 people for trafficking at least 34 babies, with 12 sent to Singapore for adoption. Authorities in both countries must now decide whether the children will remain with their adoptive families or return to Indonesia. The verdict follows months of uncertainty for the families involved.

stories.title: Littler cruises into Matchplay semis with win over Rock
stories.summary: World number one Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continues with a 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool, as he averages 109. 57. Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continued with a straightforward 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool. Rock initially kept pace with the world number one as the players traded breaks to reach 5-5. But after 19-year-old Littler reeled off five legs in a row, there proved to be no way back for Rock as the defending champion, in dazzling form, cruised into the semi-finals. After throwing 17 180s with a scoring average of 109. 57, Littler said: "At the minute, everything's going to plan.

stories.title: The Art of the Giveaway
stories.summary: W hat kind of negotiator gives the other side everything that it wants with the hope of eventually getting something in return? It’s a question that even some of President Trump’s own allies are asking after his administration signed a landmark deal to develop a civilian nuclear program in Saudi Arabia using American technology. The deal’s opponents, in the United States and Israel, worry that the new pact might ultimately allow for uranium enrichment—and if so, that Trump just gave Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman the tools he needs to build a bomb. What did the U. S. require in return? Pretty much zip. That, at least, is how things stood when the agreement was finalized yesterday.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: Google has been fined €890m by the European Union for allegedly favouring its own apps over rivals, marking the first major enforcement under the EU’s Digital Markets Act. The Commission ruled Google’s practices restricted consumer choice and gave its services an unfair advantage. Google disputes the decision, warning the ruling could strip away key features for European users.

stories.title: In video: Turkish vendor promises to give away half a tonne of watermelon if Salah deal is completed
stories.summary: A Turkish fruit vendor has promised to give away 500 kilograms of watermelon for free if Mohamed Salah signs with Besiktas. Reports indicate the Egyptian star is close to joining the Turkish club after leaving Liverpool this summer. Besiktas are finalizing details with Salah’s agent, with a deal expected soon.

stories.title: US health panel recommends easing restrictions on controversial peptides
stories.summary: A panel of federal health advisers on Thursday narrowly recommended easing access to several peptides popular with wellness influencers and celebrities, despite warnings from government scientists that the chemicals have not been shown to be safe or effective. In a series of votes, outside experts to the Food and Drug Administration advised the agency to drop current restrictions barring pharmacies from producing injectable peptides with names like BPC-157, TB-500 and KPV. The panel’s vote is not binding but is likely to embolden the health secretary, Robert F Kennedy Jr , who has described himself as a “big fan” of peptides and vowed to overturn FDA restrictions put in place under Joe Biden.

stories.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
stories.summary: The Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow featured a surprise appearance by King Charles III, drawing thunderous applause as he entered in a Tardis-like police box. Scottish athletes led the parade in kilts, followed by a Loch Ness Monster float and a rousing rendition of Flower of Scotland. The night ended with performances by Nathan Evans and Callum Beattie, celebrating Glasgow’s culture before the Games begin.

stories.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
stories.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

stories.title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
stories.summary: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

stories.title: Murder of Thomas in Crépol: the court upholds the aggravating circumstance of racism – Interview
stories.summary: The investigating judge in Valence has reclassified the charges against the suspects in the murder of Thomas Perotto in Crépol. The magistrates have added an aggravating circumstance to the charges against the accused. This decision comes after lengthy debates that have characterized the investigation since its inception. A debated legal classification The aggravating circumstance cited relates to the fact that the victims were allegedly attacked because they were white and French citizens. This legal classification had been the subject of much debate since the beginning of the investigation. Thomas Perotto was killed in 2023 in an event that deeply shocked the public.

stories.title: What to Know About Peptides the FDA Is Considering for Use
stories.summary: The agency is considering whether compounding pharmacies should be allowed to produce BPC-157, TB-500 and other previously banned peptides.

stories.title: How NEC Nijmegen could reach the UEFA Champions League for the first time
stories.summary: NEC Nijmegen had an unbelievable campaign that saw them reach the UEFA Champions League qualifiers for the first time. Check here who they'll face on their way to the league phase NEC Nijmegen 's third-place finish in the 2025/26 Eredivisie was the best in club history and earned them their first-ever spot in the UEFA Champions League qualifiers. The success is all self-made, engineered by manager Dick Schreuder, who rocked the Eredivisie with his aggressive attacking brand of football.

stories.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
stories.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

stories.title: Alan Hand becomes new President of Tullamore Lions Club
stories.summary: Dymphna Bracken and Alan Hand. Published: Thu 23 Jul 2026, 4:18 PM Alan Hand has been elected as the 52nd President of Tullamore Lions Club, succeeding outgoing president Dymphna Bracken. The official handover took place during the club’s annual changeover meeting at Giltraps of Kinnitty on Tuesday evening, where members gathered to mark the occasion and celebrate the club’s achievements over the past year. Alan previously served as the vice-president during the 25/26 term. Paying tribute to the outgoing president, the club thanked Dymphna for her commitment during her term. “We would like to thank Dymphna for her trojan work in the last 12 months,” the club said in a statement.

stories.title: Magnicity unveils reimagined euromast to boost rotterdams appeal for international travellers
stories.summary: Experiential 2 min Read Magnicity has reopened the renovated Euromast in Rotterdam following an extensive refurbishment. The transformed attraction introduces immersive storytelling, upgraded dining and enhanced observation experiences while targeting growing international markets, including India. The company aims to position the landmark as a key addition to premium leisure and MICE itineraries across the Netherlands. Magnicity has unveiled the newly renovated Euromast in Rotterdam, introducing a refreshed visitor experience that combines immersive attractions, upgraded hospitality and panoramic views of the Dutch city.

stories.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
stories.summary: Activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike after India’s government guaranteed no legal action against protesters at Jantar Mantar. The decision follows a police crackdown on Monday’s march to parliament that injured scores of demonstrators and officers. Youth-led movement CJP, which began as an online satire against exam irregularities, now leads ongoing protests demanding Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation.

stories.title: U.S. Withdraws Subpoenas Issued to New York Times Journalists
stories.summary: A federal judge raised questions about the government’s handling of the subpoenas, which The Times called an attempt to intimidate the free press.

stories.title: Health Care Agency Discontinues Dozens of Active Research Awards
stories.summary: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality slowed the awarding of new grants last year. Now it is stopping existing ones.
entities: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality

stories.title: Microsoft responds to LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows
stories.summary: Microsoft has stopped the LG Monitor App Installer from pushing McAfee ads to Windows after complaints about unauthorized installations and data collection. The app, which gained permissions to access sensitive system resources, has been active for years and sparked concerns over privacy and forced ads. Microsoft says it is reviewing its policies for peripheral companion apps.

stories.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
stories.summary: Hull KR beat local rivals Hull FC 28-20 in a tense Super League derby, ending their losing streak. Hull FC, now winless in their last 11 games, suffered their sixth straight defeat. The win lifts Hull KR into fifth place in the play-off race.

stories.title: Lauterbach, Borschow promotes Alfredo Villordo to CPA
stories.summary: Lauterbach, Borschow & Co., one of Texas's leading accounting firms, promoted Alfredo Villordo to CPA status, elevating him from tax supervisor to a key leadership role in their practice.

stories.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
stories.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

stories.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
stories.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

offbeat.title: Trump scoffs at ‘affordability’ as he touts economic policies at Georgia rally
offbeat.summary: Donald Trump scoffed at the word “affordability” as a political message, describing it at a campaign rally on Wednesday as a term concocted by Democratic political consultants to criticize his presidency. Yet even as the president dismissed the notion of affordability, he devoted much of his speech at Wheeler high school to touting the bread-and-butter benefits of his economic policies. “So I just want you to remember that if you’re paying a little bit more – a lot of it’s come down now, we got it coming down very rapidly actually,” Trump said in Marietta, an Atlanta suburb, casting higher prices today as a lingering effect of the Biden administration.

offbeat.title: Tommy White — Cobb County
offbeat.summary: Follow Tommy White Get notified by email when this case is updated. I agree to receive email updates for this case. Send me other news stories that may be of interest.

offbeat.title: Ajax Turner Senior Center board splits, takes conflicting votes, starts over
offbeat.summary: After a set of meetings in which two versions of the Senior Citizens Center Board took conflicting actions, the City of Clarksville provided an update on its plans. CLARKSVILLE, TN ( CLARKSVILLE NOW ) – After a set of meetings in which two versions of the Senior Citizens Center Board took conflicting actions, with resignations and restarts, the City of Clarksville provided an update on its plans, stating it will move forward with taking over the Ajax Turner Senior Center operations. But several questions are still unanswered. The City of Clarksville said Monday the Senior Citizens Center at 953 Clark St.

offbeat.title: Javier Bardem Reveals the Perks of Working With Wife Penelope Cruz: ‘There’s a Level of Trust’
offbeat.summary: Javier Bardem revealed the advantages of collaborating with his wife, Penélope Cruz, during a screening for Cape Fear in New York on July 21. Their upcoming film Bunker, set for U.S. release in early 2027, follows an architect whose marriage is strained after he designs a billionaire’s bunker. Bardem praised Cruz’s trust and understanding, saying it streamlined their work.

offbeat.title: How Arantxa Sanchez Vicario’s $41 million tennis fortune ‘vanished’ as her life fell apart
offbeat.summary: Arantxa Sanchez Vicario, former World No.1 tennis star, claims her $41 million fortune was drained by her ex-husband, leaving her penniless. A Spanish court found both guilty of fraud in January 2024 for hiding assets to avoid debts to Banque de Luxembourg. Sanchez Vicario avoids jail by paying half her earnings to the bank.

offbeat.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
offbeat.summary: An Indonesian court has convicted 19 people for trafficking at least 34 babies, with 12 sent to Singapore for adoption. Authorities in both countries must now decide whether the children will remain with their adoptive families or return to Indonesia. The verdict follows months of uncertainty for the families involved.

offbeat.title: The Art of the Giveaway
offbeat.summary: W hat kind of negotiator gives the other side everything that it wants with the hope of eventually getting something in return? It’s a question that even some of President Trump’s own allies are asking after his administration signed a landmark deal to develop a civilian nuclear program in Saudi Arabia using American technology. The deal’s opponents, in the United States and Israel, worry that the new pact might ultimately allow for uranium enrichment—and if so, that Trump just gave Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman the tools he needs to build a bomb. What did the U. S. require in return? Pretty much zip. That, at least, is how things stood when the agreement was finalized yesterday.

offbeat.title: In video: Turkish vendor promises to give away half a tonne of watermelon if Salah deal is completed
offbeat.summary: A Turkish fruit vendor has promised to give away 500 kilograms of watermelon for free if Mohamed Salah signs with Besiktas. Reports indicate the Egyptian star is close to joining the Turkish club after leaving Liverpool this summer. Besiktas are finalizing details with Salah’s agent, with a deal expected soon.

offbeat.title: Murder of Thomas in Crépol: the court upholds the aggravating circumstance of racism – Interview
offbeat.summary: The investigating judge in Valence has reclassified the charges against the suspects in the murder of Thomas Perotto in Crépol. The magistrates have added an aggravating circumstance to the charges against the accused. This decision comes after lengthy debates that have characterized the investigation since its inception. A debated legal classification The aggravating circumstance cited relates to the fact that the victims were allegedly attacked because they were white and French citizens. This legal classification had been the subject of much debate since the beginning of the investigation. Thomas Perotto was killed in 2023 in an event that deeply shocked the public.

offbeat.title: How NEC Nijmegen could reach the UEFA Champions League for the first time
offbeat.summary: NEC Nijmegen had an unbelievable campaign that saw them reach the UEFA Champions League qualifiers for the first time. Check here who they'll face on their way to the league phase NEC Nijmegen 's third-place finish in the 2025/26 Eredivisie was the best in club history and earned them their first-ever spot in the UEFA Champions League qualifiers. The success is all self-made, engineered by manager Dick Schreuder, who rocked the Eredivisie with his aggressive attacking brand of football.

offbeat.title: Alan Hand becomes new President of Tullamore Lions Club
offbeat.summary: Dymphna Bracken and Alan Hand. Published: Thu 23 Jul 2026, 4:18 PM Alan Hand has been elected as the 52nd President of Tullamore Lions Club, succeeding outgoing president Dymphna Bracken. The official handover took place during the club’s annual changeover meeting at Giltraps of Kinnitty on Tuesday evening, where members gathered to mark the occasion and celebrate the club’s achievements over the past year. Alan previously served as the vice-president during the 25/26 term. Paying tribute to the outgoing president, the club thanked Dymphna for her commitment during her term. “We would like to thank Dymphna for her trojan work in the last 12 months,” the club said in a statement.

offbeat.title: Magnicity unveils reimagined euromast to boost rotterdams appeal for international travellers
offbeat.summary: Experiential 2 min Read Magnicity has reopened the renovated Euromast in Rotterdam following an extensive refurbishment. The transformed attraction introduces immersive storytelling, upgraded dining and enhanced observation experiences while targeting growing international markets, including India. The company aims to position the landmark as a key addition to premium leisure and MICE itineraries across the Netherlands. Magnicity has unveiled the newly renovated Euromast in Rotterdam, introducing a refreshed visitor experience that combines immersive attractions, upgraded hospitality and panoramic views of the Dutch city.

offbeat.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
offbeat.summary: Activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike after India’s government guaranteed no legal action against protesters at Jantar Mantar. The decision follows a police crackdown on Monday’s march to parliament that injured scores of demonstrators and officers. Youth-led movement CJP, which began as an online satire against exam irregularities, now leads ongoing protests demanding Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation.

offbeat.title: Microsoft responds to LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows
offbeat.summary: Microsoft has stopped the LG Monitor App Installer from pushing McAfee ads to Windows after complaints about unauthorized installations and data collection. The app, which gained permissions to access sensitive system resources, has been active for years and sparked concerns over privacy and forced ads. Microsoft says it is reviewing its policies for peripheral companion apps.

offbeat.title: Lauterbach, Borschow promotes Alfredo Villordo to CPA
offbeat.summary: Lauterbach, Borschow & Co., one of Texas's leading accounting firms, promoted Alfredo Villordo to CPA status, elevating him from tax supervisor to a key leadership role in their practice.

breaking.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
breaking.summary: The US is imposing new tariffs of 10% to 12.5% on imports from around 60 trading partners, including the UK, China, the EU, Canada, Japan and India, starting Friday. The move targets countries accused of failing to stop forced labour, escalating trade tensions after a Supreme Court ruling blocked earlier tariffs. The White House says the duties aim to address human rights abuses and unfair trade practices.

entertainment.title: Men Flock to Microdramas, Romance Stories Rule and Sunday Nights are Prime Time for Viewing
entertainment.summary: Microdrama viewership has surged 28-fold in two years, with romance stories dominating and Sunday nights between 8-10 p.m. drawing the biggest crowds. Over two-thirds of users, now including more men, watch these short-form series, and heavy viewers show surprising openness to AI-generated content. The trend reflects a younger, more engaged audience reshaping digital entertainment.

entertainment.title: ‘Marvelous Mrs. Maisel’ Set Decorator Mary Kate Golding Fatally Stabbed in N.Y.
entertainment.summary: A set decorator for The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel and other shows was fatally stabbed in her Queens, New York, apartment on July 21, 2026. Mary Kate Golding, 34, was pronounced dead at the scene, and her husband, Joseph Azzaretto, was later found dead by hanging in Astoria Park. Police suspect a murder-suicide after responding to two separate 911 calls that morning.

entertainment.title: ‘Ebenezer: A Christmas Carol’ Trailer: Johnny Depp Transforms Into Grouchy, Snowball-Throwing Scrooge
entertainment.summary: Johnny Depp stars as Ebenezer Scrooge in the dark holiday reimagining Ebenezer: A Christmas Carol, his first major Hollywood role since his 2022 defamation trial. Directed by Ti West, the trailer shows Scrooge haunted by spirits and hurling snowballs in a bleak, snow-covered London. The film arrives as Depp returns to the screen following his high-profile legal battle with Amber Heard.

facts.title: Heatwave anxiety can make you feel 'incredibly helpless' - here's how to manage it
facts.summary: Extreme heat can take a toll on mental health - and young people and women are among the groups most at risk, according to one study. At the peak of the June heatwave, the warmest June on record in England, all Tavia Watts could think about was how hot it was in her two- and five-year-old's bedroom - above 30C in some cases. "During the heatwave, I've got a high level of anxiety all the time and I'm pretty much useless," she told the BBC. "I've had to leave bedtime when my husband reads [my kids] stories to have a little cry and to calm down, because it's just too hot in their room and I'm afraid for them.

facts.title: Scientists find building blocks of birdsong's 'spectacular diversity'
facts.summary: Scientists in France have identified eight fundamental sound motifs—three trills, three whistles, chaotic notes and harmonies—that form the basis of all birdsong worldwide. Analysing 116,000 songs from over 3,000 species, researchers say these motifs combine to create the vast diversity of birdsong. The team now plans to use AI to explore how these patterns evolve across different regions.

facts.title: What to Know About Peptides the FDA Is Considering for Use
facts.summary: The agency is considering whether compounding pharmacies should be allowed to produce BPC-157, TB-500 and other previously banned peptides.

crisis.title: UK complacent about war threat, warns defence boss
crisis.summary: The head of Europe’s largest defence firm, BAE Systems, warns the UK is dangerously complacent about rising foreign attack risks. Dr Charles Woodburn says threats are at their highest in decades and calls for faster spending to meet the 3.5% of GDP defence target by 2035. He also cautions that autonomous weapons could soon be deployed by states ignoring human control rules.

crisis.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
crisis.summary: A top US science adviser accused China’s Moonshot AI of large-scale theft, saying it used weaker models to extract capabilities from Anthropic’s Fable AI for its K3 system. The adviser also claimed Moonshot accessed restricted Nvidia servers to train its models, a charge the company has not commented on. The K3 model was unveiled last week, drawing global attention for closing the gap with Western AI systems.

crisis.title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
crisis.summary: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

sports.title: Littler cruises into Matchplay semis with win over Rock
sports.summary: World number one Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continues with a 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool, as he averages 109. 57. Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continued with a straightforward 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool. Rock initially kept pace with the world number one as the players traded breaks to reach 5-5. But after 19-year-old Littler reeled off five legs in a row, there proved to be no way back for Rock as the defending champion, in dazzling form, cruised into the semi-finals. After throwing 17 180s with a scoring average of 109. 57, Littler said: "At the minute, everything's going to plan.

sports.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
sports.summary: The Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow featured a surprise appearance by King Charles III, drawing thunderous applause as he entered in a Tardis-like police box. Scottish athletes led the parade in kilts, followed by a Loch Ness Monster float and a rousing rendition of Flower of Scotland. The night ended with performances by Nathan Evans and Callum Beattie, celebrating Glasgow’s culture before the Games begin.

sports.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
sports.summary: Hull KR beat local rivals Hull FC 28-20 in a tense Super League derby, ending their losing streak. Hull FC, now winless in their last 11 games, suffered their sixth straight defeat. The win lifts Hull KR into fifth place in the play-off race.

tech.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
tech.summary: Google has been fined €890m by the European Union for allegedly favouring its own apps over rivals, marking the first major enforcement under the EU’s Digital Markets Act. The Commission ruled Google’s practices restricted consumer choice and gave its services an unfair advantage. Google disputes the decision, warning the ruling could strip away key features for European users.

tech.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
tech.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

tech.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
tech.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

health.title: US health panel recommends easing restrictions on controversial peptides
health.summary: A panel of federal health advisers on Thursday narrowly recommended easing access to several peptides popular with wellness influencers and celebrities, despite warnings from government scientists that the chemicals have not been shown to be safe or effective. In a series of votes, outside experts to the Food and Drug Administration advised the agency to drop current restrictions barring pharmacies from producing injectable peptides with names like BPC-157, TB-500 and KPV. The panel’s vote is not binding but is likely to embolden the health secretary, Robert F Kennedy Jr , who has described himself as a “big fan” of peptides and vowed to overturn FDA restrictions put in place under Joe Biden.

health.title: Health Care Agency Discontinues Dozens of Active Research Awards
health.summary: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality slowed the awarding of new grants last year. Now it is stopping existing ones.
entities: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality

health.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
health.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

politics.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
politics.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

politics.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
politics.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

politics.title: U.S. Withdraws Subpoenas Issued to New York Times Journalists
politics.summary: A federal judge raised questions about the government’s handling of the subpoenas, which The Times called an attempt to intimidate the free press.
